data.title: U Power files to sell 1.56M ordinary shares by selling shareholders
data.description:
• U Power (NASDAQ:UCAR) filed a prospectus related to the offer and resale of up to about 1.56 million shares of class A ordinary shares.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of ordinary shares by the selling shareholder.
  • Filing

data.title: ChargePoint receives non-compliance notice with NYSE listing rule
data.description:
ChargePoint (NYSE:CHPT) said on Friday that it was notified by the New York Stock Exchange that it is not in compliance with NYSE listing rule because the average closing price of the company’s common stock was less than $1.00 over a consecutive 30 trading-day period, which is the minimum average closing price required to maintain continued listing on the NYSE.

The notice is a notice of deficiency, not delisting, and does not currently affect the listing or trading of the company’s common stock on the NYSE.

The company plans to notify the NYSE by March 5, 2025, that it intends to cure the average closing stock price deficiency and to return to compliance with the NYSE's continued listing standards.

The company intends to consider available alternatives, including but not limited to, a reverse stock split, subject to stockholder approval no later than at the company’s next annual meeting of stockholders, if necessary, to cure the stock price non-compliance.

Furthermore, the notice is not anticipated to impact the ongoing business operations of the company or its reporting requirements with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

ChargePoint (NYSE:CHPT) shares were down 1.33% in extended trading on Friday.

data.title: Nasdaq ends 2% lower as gloomy economic data fuel U.S. stocks to weekly losses
data.description:
U.S. stocks tumbled Friday, as investors closed the week with gloomy economic updates and word of a government investigation into UnitedHealth (UNH) in hand.

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -2.2%, its steepest fall since late January. The Dow (DJI) tanked 749 points, or -1.7%. The S&P 500 (SP500) -1.7%, to 6,013.13, the lowest end since early February. Wall Street's key volatility gauge (VIX) spiked up 16%, rising above 18 for the first time in nearly two weeks, with the session marking February options expirations.

“Today, risk-blind investors were given a stern talking-to by the market. Low demand for S&P 500 (SP500)puts in recent weeks, as demonstrated by the VIX (VIX) hugging support since early February, led to a dash for the door during today’s monthly options expiry re-hedging flows," Alex King, investing group leader of Cestrian Capital Research, told Seeking Alpha.

10 of the 11 sectors on the S&P 500 (SP500) fell, led by Consumer Discretionary (XLY) -2.8%. Consumer Staples (XLP) was the sole advancer, +1.0%.

UnitedHealth (UNH) led decliners on the Dow (DJI). Shares dropped -7.3% after The Wall Street Journal reported the U.S. Department of Justice has launched an investigation into the company’s Medicare billing practices. UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) declined to comment, and the company later criticized the WSJ over its reporting.

Investors also assessed dour economic reports. “U.S. business activity growth came close to stalling in February as a renewed fall in services output offset faster manufacturing growth,” S&P Global Market Intelligence said. Its February S&P Composite PMI fell to 51.6 from the 52.7 prior print. The services index dropped to 49.7 from 53.0.

“The upbeat mood seen among U.S. businesses at the start of the year has evaporated, replaced with a darkening picture of heightened uncertainty,” Chris Williamson, chief business economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence, said in the report.

Also, the University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index fell to 64.7 in February's final reading, missing the 68.0 consensus, clipped by worries that tariff-induced price increases are approaching.

Separately, January U.S. existing home sales slumped -4.9% to 4.08M versus the 4.13M projection.

The Dow (DJI) for the holiday-shortened week fell 2.5%. The S&P 500 (SP500) this week notched two record closing highs, but ended 1.7% lower for the week.

Investors flocked to safety in the bond market, driving the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) down further, by 8 basis points to 4.43%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 7 basis points to 4.21%.

U.S. stocks sold-off Thursday, in part as investors balked at Walmart's (WMT) soft financial outlook.

data.title: TXNM Energy outlines 7%-9% EPS growth through 2029 with $7.8B investment plan
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TXNM Energy, Inc. (NYSE:TXNM) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Pat Vincent-Collawn announced that ongoing earnings for 2024 were $2.74 per share, hitting the high end of guidance. The company introduced 2025 guidance in the range of $2.74 to $2.84 per share, reflecting growth in Texas and benefits from an unopposed rate settlement in New Mexico.
  • The five-year investment plan for Texas increased by over $1 billion, now targeting $7.8 billion through 2029. This supports an updated EPS growth target of 7%-9% through 2029.
  • The board approved a 5% dividend increase, maintaining a payout ratio of 50%-60% for 2025.
  • COO Don Tarry highlighted TNMP's strong growth, including an 18% increase in system peaks over 2023, driven by growth in data center demand. Interconnection requests rose 10% in 2024 compared to 2023.
  • CFO Lisa Eden noted that the capital plan has grown to $7.8 billion, up $1.6 billion from prior estimates. "We are confident in our ability to achieve these targets and create value for shareholders," she added.

Outlook

  • Management introduced EPS guidance for 2025 between $2.74 and $2.84. This includes phased rate increases in New Mexico and ongoing recovery of investments in Texas through TCOS and DCRF mechanisms.
  • The five-year capital plan now includes $7.8 billion in investments, driven by high growth in Texas and new transmission projects in New Mexico.
  • The company reaffirmed its long-term EPS growth target of 7%-9%, supported by new investments and regulatory developments.

Financial Results

  • 2024 earnings per share reached $2.74, the high end of guidance. Key contributors included retail load growth at PNM, capital recovery through TCOS and DCRF mechanisms, and improved decommissioning trust performance.
  • Higher depreciation, property tax, and interest expenses from new investments, along with share dilution from 2023 equity issuances, partially offset earnings growth.
  • The $7.8 billion capital plan supports rate base growth for TNMP and PNM. TNMP’s rate base is expected to grow by 17% through 2029, surpassing PNM Retail by 2027.

Q&A

  • Julien Dumoulin-Smith, Jefferies: Asked about incremental opportunities beyond the current plan, including the Permian Basin projects and potential scenarios. COO Don Tarry responded that ERCOT's 345 kV voltage path could add $900 million in incremental capital.
  • Michael Lonegan, Evercore ISI: Inquired about capital structure expectations for the upcoming Texas rate case. COO Tarry stated they are "cognizant of legislative proposals" and will balance distribution and transmission elements in the filing.
  • Andrew Weisel, Scotiabank: Asked about the wider range in the EPS CAGR. CFO Lisa Eden explained the broader range reflects the scale of the capital plan and associated opportunities.
  • Ryan Levine, Citi: Questioned the timeline for addressing Four Corners’ replacement capacity. COO Tarry stated the replacement process is underway with results from the RFP expected mid-2025.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts conveyed a positive tone overall, frequently commending the clarity of the updated EPS targets and the significant investment plan. Questions focused on upside opportunities and strategic execution, indicating confidence in the company’s direction.
  • Management maintained an optimistic tone during the prepared remarks, with phrases like "our future is so bright" and "we are confident." During the Q&A, responses remained consistent and detail-oriented, indicating preparedness.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone reflected greater confidence, particularly with the expanded five-year investment plan and solid regulatory outcomes.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The EPS growth target increased from 6%-7% to 7%-9%, reflecting higher capital investments and growth opportunities, particularly in Texas.
  • Dividend growth remained at 5%, aligning with guidance provided in prior quarters.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from general regulatory updates in Q3 to detailed questions about investment execution and upside potential in Q4.
  • Management’s confidence improved, supported by strong financial results and constructive outcomes in rate cases.

Risks and Concerns

  • Challenges include regulatory uncertainty in Texas, particularly around proposed legislation affecting utility capital structures.
  • The company faces execution risks in managing $7.8 billion in capital investments while balancing customer affordability.
  • Delays in legislative approval for mobile generation in Texas could impact project timelines.
  • Potential equity issuance requirements to finance growth could weigh on shareholder returns if not optimized.

Final Takeaway

TXNM Energy delivered strong financial results in 2024 and set ambitious targets for the next five years, including a $7.8 billion capital plan and a 7%-9% EPS growth target through 2029. The company’s strategic focus on high-growth areas like Texas, alongside its constructive regulatory outcomes, positions it well for sustained shareholder value creation. While execution risks and regulatory challenges remain, management expressed confidence in its ability to navigate these hurdles effectively.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Air Liquide sees just two of six U.S. hydrogen hubs moving ahead after Trump ends funding
data.description:
Air Liquide (OTCPK:AIQUF) (OTCPK:AIQUY) said Friday it expects only two out of six previously awarded hydrogen projects for the U.S. Department of Energy may move forward after President Trump ended funding for clean energy projects.

Air Liquide (OTCPK:AIQUF) (OTCPK:AIQUY) was selected in 2023 as a partner for six clean hydrogen hubs planned by the Biden administration's DoE, which had planned to allocate $7B for the projects, but Trump has placed holds on billions of dollars in spending, including from the Inflation Reduction Act.

"After meeting with the American teams a few days ago, two of the hubs around hydrogen from natural gas with carbon capture are on top of the list to be pursued," while plans for the other four hubs will depend largely on the new administration's decisions on renewable energy policies, CEO François Jackow said on the company's earnings conference call.

Air Liquide (OTCPK:AIQUF) (OTCPK:AIQUY) has been a major investor in the U.S. clean energy transition, including a partnership with Exxon Mobil (XOM) to enhance the production of low-carbon hydrogen and ammonia at the oil company's Baytown, Texas, facility; Jackow said that while the project with Exxon was not "absolutely decided yet," the companyies are continuing to pursue it, with a final decision expected in this year's H2.

The CEO said Trump's potential tariffs would have "very little impact" on Air Liquide's (OTCPK:AIQUF) (OTCPK:AIQUY) U.S. business, which makes up roughly a third of its total sales, thanks to local sourcing of materials.

data.title: Guardant Health files lawsuit against Natera for stealing trade secrets
data.description:
• Cancer test maker Guardant Health (NASDAQ:GH) has filed a lawsuit in a California district court this week alleging that its rival Natera (NASDAQ:NTRA) stole its trade secrets by recruiting its scientists, whom it said had illegally transferred company data before their resignations.
  • In the lawsuit filed at the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California on Thursday, the company argued that “unable to compete fairly, Natera recently turned its focus on not only poaching Guardant’s employees but stealing Guardant’s trade secrets.”
  • GH alleged that two of its employees, Alan Selewa and Catalin Barbacioru, resigned this year to join Natera (NASDAQ:NTRA) but not before illegally transferring more than 6,500 key trade secrets from its IT system.
  • The company seeks a court order forcing a return of all transferred trade secret information and unspecified monetary damages.

data.title: Twin Peaks is in the spotlight as Hooters heads toward a bankruptcy filing
data.description:
Hooters of America is reportedly working with creditors on a plan to restructure the business through bankruptcy court. Sources told Bloomberg that they expect the bankruptcy process to formally begin in the next few months.

The restaurant company operates the Hooters chain as a sports bar concept that features skimpy server uniforms. The chain was founded in Clearwater, Florida, by six businessmen with no prior restaurant experience. The concept combined casual dining, sports, and attractive waitresses known as "Hooters Girls". Initially struggling, the restaurant gained popularity after NFL players visited during the 1984 Super Bowl. The chain expanded throughout the U.S. and internationally in the 1980s and 1990s before expansion slowed down.

The Hooters development could be relevant for Twin Hospitality Group (NASDAQ:TWNP), which was recently spun off from FAT Brands (FAT). The company runs the Twin Peaks chain, which competes directly for sales against Hooters and other restaurant operators such as Darden Restaurants (DRI), Brinker International (EAT), Buffalo Wild Wings, Tilted Kilt, and Wingstop (WING).

data.title: Atmus projects $1.67B-$1.735B in 2025 revenue with 19%-20% EBITDA margin target
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Atmus Filtration Technologies (NYSE:ATMU) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Steph Disher highlighted the company's strong financial performance in Q4 2024, with sales reaching $407 million, supported by higher volumes and pricing actions. The company also completed $20 million in share repurchases during the year and has $130 million authorized for 2025.
  • The company emphasized progress on its 4-pillar growth strategy, including a new business win with a major European OEM for fuel filtration and crankcase ventilation, and the launch of the NanoNet N3 media technology to expand fuel filtration capabilities.
  • Operational separation from Cummins is 95% complete, with full independence expected in 2025. Adjusted EBITDA margin expanded by 410 basis points since 2022, supported by supply chain transformation and cost reduction efforts.
  • CFO Jack Kienzler reported adjusted EBITDA of $78 million in Q4, representing 19.1% of sales, compared to 17.9% in the previous year. Adjusted EPS for the quarter was $0.58, and adjusted free cash flow was $28 million.

Outlook

  • Atmus anticipates 2025 revenue in the range of $1.67 billion to $1.735 billion, reflecting a flat to 4% growth compared to 2024. Adjusted EBITDA margin is projected between 19% and 20%.
  • Adjusted EPS for 2025 is expected to range between $2.35 and $2.60.
  • The company expects aftermarket revenue to be flat to up 3%, supported by a 2% market outperformance and a 1% pricing increase, offset by a 2% headwind from a strong U.S. dollar.
  • First-fit markets are expected to face challenging conditions, with U.S. heavy-duty production flat to down 10%, and medium-duty production down 5% to 15%. India and China markets are anticipated to remain weak.

Financial Results

  • Sales in Q4 2024 increased by 1.8% year-over-year to $407 million, driven by a 2% increase in volumes and a 1% pricing benefit, partially offset by a 1% foreign exchange headwind.
  • Gross margin reached $107 million, supported by lower manufacturing costs, though higher logistics and material costs partially offset these benefits.
  • For the full year 2024, sales rose 2.5% to $1.67 billion, with adjusted EBITDA reaching $330 million, equating to a 19.7% margin. Adjusted EPS for the year was $2.03, and adjusted free cash flow totaled $115 million.
  • One-time costs related to separation from Cummins amounted to $25 million for the year, with an additional $5 million to $10 million expected in 2025.

Q&A

  • Joe O'Dea, Wells Fargo: Questioned the sustainability of EBITDA margin improvement and quarterly cadence for 2025. CFO Jack Kienzler explained that foreign exchange headwinds, rising input costs, and a lag in pricing adjustments could impact early 2025 margins, with sequential improvements expected later in the year.
  • Rob Mason, Baird: Asked about seasonality and first-half revenue weighting. CEO Steph Disher noted that aftermarket recovery and first-fit market rebounds are more likely in the second half of 2025, shifting traditional seasonality patterns.
  • Bobby Brooks, Northland Capital Markets: Inquired about mitigating tariff impacts on manufacturing in China and Mexico. Disher outlined pricing actions and supply chain flexibility as key strategies.
  • Jerry Revich, Goldman Sachs: Asked about incremental margin expansion opportunities. Disher stated that the focus is on sustaining the 19%-20% EBITDA margin range while driving growth through supply chain transformation and value engineering.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on margin sustainability, growth initiatives, and market recovery timelines. Questions reflected cautious optimism about the company's ability to navigate challenging market conditions.
  • Management displayed confidence during prepared remarks, emphasizing strategic wins and financial discipline. However, during exchanges with analysts, the tone shifted slightly defensive when addressing market headwinds and tariff-related uncertainties.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a consistent level of confidence but provided more detailed explanations regarding the impacts of macroeconomic pressures.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 revenue ($1.67 billion to $1.735 billion) showed an increase over the previous quarter's outlook for 2024 ($1.65 billion to $1.675 billion).
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance remained consistent at 19%-20%, compared to the prior quarter's raised range of 19.25%-19.75% for 2024.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from transition-related costs in Q3 to broader market recovery timelines and tariff impacts in Q4.
  • Management tone remained confident but included more granular insights into supply chain and pricing strategies.

Risks and Concerns

  • Weak market conditions in China and India are expected to persist into 2025, with limited visibility into recovery timelines.
  • Foreign exchange headwinds and rising input costs, particularly steel, were highlighted as key challenges.
  • Tariff uncertainties, particularly related to U.S.-Mexico trade, could impact manufacturing costs and supply chain dynamics.
  • Restructuring actions in China and the U.S. signal ongoing adjustments to navigate soft market conditions.

Final Takeaway

Atmus Filtration Technologies delivered solid Q4 2024 results and outlined a cautious yet optimistic outlook for 2025, projecting revenue growth and sustained strong margins despite challenging market conditions. Key strategic initiatives, including investments in technology, supply chain transformation, and targeted market expansion, are expected to support resilience and growth. However, analysts raised concerns about macroeconomic headwinds, tariff uncertainties, and the timing of market recoveries, underscoring the importance of execution in the coming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Apple Intelligence plans to add more languages and make its debut in EU
data.description:
Apple Intelligence (NASDAQ:AAPL), the tech giant's personalized AI system, is being introduced in several new languages and is finally coming to European Union nations, beginning in April, the company announced today.

The new languages include French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Spanish, Korean and Chinese. It will also be released in localized English versions for Singapore and India.

The new languages will coincide with the release of a series of operating system updates, including iOS 18.4, iPadOS 18.4 and macOS Sequoia 15.4. These will be released today for developers.

The updates will also bring Apple Intelligence to iPhone and iPad users in European Union nations for the first time. Apple Intelligence features will expand to the Vision Pro as well.
